mnemonics: S.A.D. Models: Select Appropriate Data. Remember that optimizing for Sentiment Analysis means choosing the right dataset, architecture, and deployment strategy to avoid a sad model.
keyPoints: Fine-tuning a pre-trained language model (like BERT) on a domain-specific dataset is crucial for high accuracy.
Class imbalance (e.g., far more positive reviews than negative) is a common problem that requires techniques like weighted loss functions or oversampling.
Deploying a model requires considering inference speed and cost, often leading to a choice between a large, accurate model and a smaller, distilled one.
commonMistakes: Using a general-purpose sentiment model on a domain with specific jargon (e.g., financial news or product reviews) without fine-tuning.
Ignoring class imbalance during training, which causes the model to be biased towards the majority class and perform poorly on the minority class.
Evaluating a model only on overall accuracy instead of using metrics like Precision, Recall, and F1-score for each sentiment class.
